---
title: "Research"
pagetitle: "Research — Werner L. Hernani-Limarino"
description: "Research program on poverty measurement, machine learning for poverty targeting and small area estimation, distributional incidence analysis, structural poverty and informality in Bolivia, intergenerational mobility, inequality, social protection, and gender norms in Latin America and the Caribbean."
toc: true
toc-title: "Themes"
toc-depth: 2
---

Working papers, submitted manuscripts, and publications organized by research theme. Papers marked *Under review* or *Submitted* are available on SSRN or upon request.

---

## 1. Income Measurement and the Invisible Firm {#income-measurement}

::: {.theme-block}
::: {.theme-desc}
Traditional survey instruments designed for wage workers produce income figures for the self-employed that reverse sign for the majority of informal firms once full costs are accounted for. I develop gold-standard diary validation, ML-based corrections, and structural approaches for contexts where only consumption is observed.
:::

<ul>
<li>
*Ledgers of the Self-Employed: Accounting for the Invisible Firm*
<span class="paper-links">
<a href="https://github.com/wernerhl/ledgers-bolivia" target="_blank">GitHub</a>
</span>
<span class="paper-status">— Working Paper</span>
</li>
<li>
*The Income You Can't See: Detecting and Correcting Self-Employment Measurement Error with Unsupervised Machine Learning*
<span class="paper-links">
<a href="https://github.com/wernerhl/working-papers/raw/main/income_you_cant_see.pdf" target="_blank">PDF</a>
</span>
<span class="paper-status">— Submitted, *Journal of Development Economics*</span>
</li>
<li>
*The Noise Floor: Measurement Error Limits on Poverty Mapping and a Machine Learning Solution*
<span class="paper-status">— Working Paper</span>
</li>
<li>
*Recovering Individual Labor Earnings from Household Consumption: A Collective Model Approach with an Application to the Sahel G5*
<span class="paper-links">
<a href="https://ssrn.com/abstract=6332779" target="_blank">SSRN</a>
</span>
<span class="paper-status">— Working Paper</span>
</li>
<li>
*Identification and Estimation of Income Tails Under Nonclassical Measurement Error: Evidence from Brazil*
<span class="paper-status">— Working Paper</span>
</li>
</ul>
:::

---

## 2. Optimal Poverty Targeting {#optimal-targeting}

::: {.theme-block}
::: {.theme-desc}
Proxy means tests and poverty maps break down where poverty populations concentrate — in the distribution tails. I develop augmented ML methods and small area estimation techniques that remain reliable where standard tools fail, validated across 17 LAC countries.
:::

<ul>
<li>
*Optimal Poverty Prediction Under Measurement Error: Theory and Evidence from Augmented Machine Learning*
<span class="paper-links">
<a href="https://ssrn.com/abstract=6332698" target="_blank">SSRN</a>
</span>
<span class="paper-status">— Review & Resubmit, *Journal of Applied Econometrics*</span>
</li>
<li>
*Poverty Mapping in the Tails*
<span class="paper-links">
<a href="https://ssrn.com/abstract=6338941" target="_blank">SSRN</a>
</span>
<span class="paper-status">— Submitted, *Journal of Econometrics*</span>
</li>
<li>
*The Tail Test: A Factorial Simulation on the Sources of DRF Advantage in Small Area Estimation*
<span class="paper-links">
<a href="https://github.com/wernerhl/tail-test-replication" target="_blank">GitHub</a>
</span>
<span class="paper-status">— Submitted, *Journal of the Royal Statistical Society, Series A*</span>
</li>
<li>
*Data Augmentation for Bias Reduction in Imbalanced Regression*
<span class="paper-links">
<a href="https://github.com/wernerhl/working-papers/raw/main/data_augmentation.pdf" target="_blank">PDF</a>
</span>
<span class="paper-status">— Working Paper</span>
</li>
</ul>
:::

---

## 3. Distributional Incidence Analysis {#distributional-incidence}

::: {.theme-block}
::: {.theme-desc}
When governments reform subsidies, currencies collapse, or climate shocks hit, who gains and who loses? I develop real-time distributional monitoring tools combining microsimulation, ML filters, and minimum cross-entropy reweighting to estimate welfare impacts between survey rounds.
:::

<ul>
<li>
*Between Surveys: Real-Time Distributional Monitoring for Vulnerable Populations*
<span class="paper-status">— Working Paper</span>
</li>
<li>
*Forecasting the Distributional Impacts of Climate Change and Climate Policy*
<span class="paper-links">
<a href="https://github.com/wernerhl/working-papers/raw/main/wb_climate_microsimulation.pdf" target="_blank">PDF</a>
</span>
<span class="paper-status">— World Bank Technical Note</span>
</li>
<li>
*P2P Dollars as an Early Warning System: Exchange Market Pressure, Panic Premia, and the Currency Stress Cycle in Bolivia*
<span class="paper-links">
<a href="https://github.com/wernerhl/working-papers/raw/main/p2p_ews_bolivia.pdf" target="_blank">PDF</a>
<a href="https://wernerhl.github.io/bolivia-ews/" target="_blank">Dashboard</a>
</span>
<span class="paper-status">— Working Paper WP/2026/01</span>
</li>
<li>
*Who Bears the Cost of Devaluation? Price Dynamics and Welfare Impacts During Bolivia's 2024–2025 Currency Crisis*
<span class="paper-links">
<a href="https://doi.org/10.35319/lajed.202544558" target="_blank" class="paper-link-published">DOI</a>
<a href="https://ssrn.com/abstract=6347479" target="_blank">SSRN</a>
<a href="https://github.com/wernerhl/BOL-NoReturns" target="_blank">GitHub</a>
</span>
<span class="paper-status">— Published, *Revista Latinoamericana de Desarrollo Económico*, No. 44, 2025</span>
</li>
<li>
*When the Pump Becomes a Shock: Stress-Adjusted Incidence of Fuel Subsidy Reform in a Fragile Informal Economy*
<span class="paper-status">— Working Paper</span>
</li>
<li>
*Estimands, Specification Sensitivity, and Tail Reliability in Growth Incidence Curve Analysis*
<span class="paper-status">— Working Paper</span>
</li>
<li>
*Measuring Redistribution Effects of Climate Change*
<span class="paper-status">— Working Paper</span>
</li>
</ul>
:::

---

## 4. Structural Poverty, Informality, and Inclusion Policy {#structural-poverty}

::: {.theme-block}
::: {.theme-desc}
Not all poverty is the same. I distinguish workers structurally trapped in loss-making informal firms by accumulated relational capital from the transitory poor, and derive the distinct policy implications for social inclusion, social investment, and social protection.
:::

<ul>
<li>
*Quantifying Permanent, Structural, and Transitory Income Components with Machine Learning: Re-examining the Inequality Decline in Latin America*
<span class="paper-links">
<a href="https://github.com/wernerhl/working-papers/raw/main/structural_income_ml.pdf" target="_blank">PDF</a>
</span>
<span class="paper-status">— Working Paper</span>
</li>
<li>
*Structural Poverty in Latin America: Definition, Measurement, and Twenty Years of Change*
<span class="paper-status">— Working Paper</span>
</li>
<li>
*The Three Faces of Inequality: Systematic, Structural, and Residual Inequality in Latin America, 2000–2022*
<span class="paper-status">— Submitted, *Journal of Development Economics*</span>
</li>
<li>
*Trapped by Cash: A Theory of Voluntary Informality Among the Economically Non-Viable*
<span class="paper-links">
<a href="https://github.com/wernerhl/working-papers/raw/main/trapped_by_cash.pdf" target="_blank">PDF</a>
</span>
<span class="paper-status">— Working Paper</span>
</li>
<li>
*To Be a Taquero or to Build a Taquería: Occupational Sorting under Risk and Embedded Capital*
<span class="paper-status">— Submitted, *Journal of Development Economics*</span>
</li>
<li>
*Narcocorridos as Recruitment Technology: Cultural Prestige Production, Adverse Selection, and Criminal Human Capital in Mexico and Brazil*
<span class="paper-links">
<a href="https://ssrn.com/abstract=6419718" target="_blank">SSRN</a>
</span>
<span class="paper-status">— Working Paper</span>
</li>
</ul>
:::

---

## 5. Intergenerational Mobility and Inequality of Opportunity {#mobility}

::: {.theme-block}
::: {.theme-desc}
Falling intergenerational coefficients across LAC mask rising positional rigidity. I develop the Mobility Frontier framework and the schooling lag methodology, and apply both to Mexico, Bolivia, and 18 LAC countries using census and survey data spanning five decades.
:::

<ul>
<li>
*The Mobility Frontier: Why the Intergenerational Elasticity Is an Insufficient Statistic*
<span class="paper-links">
<a href="https://github.com/wernerhl/working-papers/raw/main/mobility_frontier.pdf" target="_blank">PDF</a>
</span>
<span class="paper-status">— Working Paper</span>
</li>
<li>
*Ladders and Gates: Where Opportunity Breaks in Mexico*
<span class="paper-links">
<a href="https://github.com/wernerhl/working-papers/raw/main/ladders_and_gates.pdf" target="_blank">PDF</a>
</span>
<span class="paper-status">— Working Paper</span>
</li>
<li>
*Sticky Ends, Mobile Middle: The Shape of Intergenerational Persistence in Mexico*
<span class="paper-status">— Submitted, *Journal of Economic Inequality*</span>
</li>
<li>
*The Schooling Lag: Rank Persistence and the Measurement of Intergenerational Educational Mobility*
<span class="paper-links">
<a href="https://github.com/wernerhl/working-papers/raw/main/schooling_lag.pdf" target="_blank">PDF</a>
</span>
<span class="paper-status">— Submitted, *Review of Income and Wealth*</span>
</li>
<li>
*Mexico's Mobility Mirage: Falling Intergenerational Coefficients, Rising Positional Rigidity*
<span class="paper-links">
<a href="https://ssrn.com/abstract=6441578" target="_blank">SSRN</a>
</span>
<span class="paper-status">— Submitted, *Journal of Development Economics*</span>
</li>
<li>
*The Road of No Returns: 25 Years of Returns to Education in Bolivia (1999–2024)*
<span class="paper-links">
<a href="https://ssrn.com/abstract=6406038" target="_blank">SSRN</a>
</span>
<span class="paper-status">— Working Paper</span>
</li>
<li>
*Between Communities and Within Them: A Five-Census Decomposition of Intergenerational Persistence in Bolivia, 1976–2024*
<span class="paper-status">— Working Paper</span>
</li>
<li>
*Network Thickness, Inequality of Opportunity, and the Persistence of Advantage*
<span class="paper-status">— Submitted, *Social Choice and Welfare*</span>
</li>
</ul>
:::

---

## 6. Gender, Adaptive Preferences, and Social Norms {#gender}

::: {.theme-block}
::: {.theme-desc}
Persistent gender inequality operates through preferences and norms, not just endowments and returns. When preferences are endogenous to the distribution, redistribution alone cannot close gaps. I develop the theory and provide intersectional evidence across LAC.
:::

<ul>
<li>
*Adaptive Preferences, Endogenous Returns, and the Failure of the Second Welfare Theorem*
<span class="paper-links">
<a href="https://ssrn.com/abstract=6325738" target="_blank">SSRN</a>
</span>
<span class="paper-status">— Working Paper</span>
</li>
<li>
*Mainstreaming the Wrong Channel: Gender Policy, Adaptive Preferences, and the Limits of Redistribution*
<span class="paper-links">
<a href="https://github.com/wernerhl/working-papers/raw/main/mainstreaming_wrong_channel.pdf" target="_blank">PDF</a>
</span>
<span class="paper-status">— Working Paper</span>
</li>
<li>
*Compounding Penalties: Gender, Ethnicity, and the Structure of Wage Inequality Among Indigenous Women in Latin America*
<span class="paper-status">— Working Paper</span>
</li>
<li>
*Adaptive Preferences, Social Filters, and the Political Economy of Gendered Inequality*
<span class="paper-links">
<a href="https://ssrn.com/abstract=6325779" target="_blank">SSRN</a>
</span>
<span class="paper-status">— Working Paper</span>
</li>
<li>
*Where Does the Money Go? Financial Inclusion, Sectoral Savings, and the Gender Misallocation Trap*
<span class="paper-links">
<a href="https://ssrn.com/abstract=6353618" target="_blank">SSRN</a>
</span>
<span class="paper-status">— Working Paper</span>
</li>
<li>
*Skills or Parks? A Nested Test of Dynamic Complementarity versus Preference Formation in Early Childhood*
<span class="paper-links">
<a href="https://github.com/wernerhl/working-papers/raw/main/skills_or_parks.pdf" target="_blank">PDF</a>
</span>
<span class="paper-status">— Working Paper</span>
</li>
</ul>
:::

---

## Bolivia: Applied Research and Live Tools {#bolivia}

::: {.theme-block}
::: {.theme-desc}
Bolivia has the highest informality rate in Latin America, a currency under stress, and some of the deepest ethnic and geographic inequality in the hemisphere. I have worked with its household surveys since 1999, its five population censuses since 1976, and its informal economy through 500 firm-level financial diaries. The tools below — a live currency early warning system, a 339-municipality poverty map, and an implicit bias experiment — are applications of the methods developed in the research program above to the country I know best.
:::

**Live dashboards and interactive tools**

<ul>
<li>
*Bolivia P2P Dollar — Early Warning System*
<span class="paper-links">
<a href="https://wernerhl.github.io/bolivia-ews/" target="_blank">Live Dashboard</a>
</span>
<span class="paper-status">Dynamic Factor Model + Kalman filter. Updated daily from Binance P2P order book.</span>
</li>
<li>
*Bolivia Poverty & Nutrition Map 2024 — 339 Municipalities*
<span class="paper-links">
<a href="https://wernerhl.github.io/poverty-map-bolivia/" target="_blank">Interactive Map</a>
</span>
<span class="paper-status">Census 2024 + DRF + BYM2 + Optimal Transport calibration. Poverty, extreme poverty, stunting, wasting, anemia.</span>
</li>
<li>
*Implicit Association Test — Racial Bias in Latin America*
<span class="paper-links">
<a href="https://wernerhl.github.io/iat-pilot/" target="_blank">Take the Test</a>
</span>
<span class="paper-status">Measures implicit bias against indigenous names and faces. 7-minute online experiment.</span>
</li>
</ul>

**Bolivia-focused papers** (cross-referenced from themes above)

<ul>
<li>
*P2P Dollars as an Early Warning System: Exchange Market Pressure, Panic Premia, and the Currency Stress Cycle in Bolivia*
<span class="paper-links">
<a href="https://github.com/wernerhl/working-papers/raw/main/p2p_ews_bolivia.pdf" target="_blank">PDF</a>
<a href="https://wernerhl.github.io/bolivia-ews/" target="_blank">Dashboard</a>
</span>
<span class="paper-status">— Working Paper · [Theme 3](#distributional-incidence)</span>
</li>
<li>
*Who Bears the Cost of Devaluation? Price Dynamics and Welfare Impacts During Bolivia's 2024–2025 Currency Crisis*
<span class="paper-links">
<a href="https://doi.org/10.35319/lajed.202544558" target="_blank" class="paper-link-published">DOI</a>
<a href="https://ssrn.com/abstract=6347479" target="_blank">SSRN</a>
</span>
<span class="paper-status">— Published, *Revista Latinoamericana de Desarrollo Económico*, 2025 · [Theme 3](#distributional-incidence)</span>
</li>
<li>
*Ledgers of the Self-Employed: Accounting for the Invisible Firm* — 300 firms, 14,902 transactions, double-entry financial diary
<span class="paper-links">
<a href="https://github.com/wernerhl/ledgers-bolivia" target="_blank">GitHub</a>
</span>
<span class="paper-status">— Working Paper · [Theme 1](#income-measurement)</span>
</li>
<li>
*Trapped by Cash: A Theory of Voluntary Informality Among the Economically Non-Viable* — 500 firms, four-type equilibrium partition
<span class="paper-links">
<a href="https://github.com/wernerhl/working-papers/raw/main/trapped_by_cash.pdf" target="_blank">PDF</a>
</span>
<span class="paper-status">— Working Paper · [Theme 4](#structural-poverty)</span>
</li>
<li>
*The Road of No Returns: 25 Years of Returns to Education in Bolivia (1999–2024)*
<span class="paper-links">
<a href="https://ssrn.com/abstract=6406038" target="_blank">SSRN</a>
</span>
<span class="paper-status">— Working Paper · [Theme 5](#mobility)</span>
</li>
<li>
*Between Communities and Within Them: A Five-Census Decomposition of Intergenerational Persistence in Bolivia, 1976–2024*
<span class="paper-status">— Working Paper · [Theme 5](#mobility)</span>
</li>
<li>
*La evolución de la pobreza en Bolivia: un enfoque multidimensional* (with P. Villarroel).
<span class="paper-links">
<a href="https://lajed.ucb.edu.bo/a/article/view/a126" target="_blank" class="paper-link-published">DOI</a>
</span>
<span class="paper-status">— *Revista Latinoamericana de Desarrollo Económico*, No. 20, 2013</span>
</li>
<li>
*¿Qué Separa a los Bolivianos? Un Estudio Experimental de los Efectos de las Identidades Sociales en la Confianza y el Afecto*
<span class="paper-links">
<a href="https://lajed.ucb.edu.bo/a/article/view/541" target="_blank" class="paper-link-published">DOI</a>
<a href="https://github.com/wernerhl/working-papers/raw/main/que_separa_bolivianos_slides.pptx" target="_blank">Slides</a>
</span>
<span class="paper-status">— *Revista Latinoamericana de Desarrollo Económico*, 2024</span>
</li>
<li>
*Understanding Changes in Social Cohesion in Bolivia* (with M.A. Villegas).
<span class="paper-links">
<a href="https://www.aru.org.bo/REPEC/pdf/understanding-changes-in-social-cohesion-bolivia.pdf" target="_blank">PDF</a>
<a href="https://github.com/wernerhl/working-papers/raw/main/social_cohesion_bolivia_slides.pptx" target="_blank">Slides</a>
</span>
<span class="paper-status">— OECD Conference on Social Cohesion and Development, 2010</span>
</li>
</ul>
:::

---

## Earlier Publications (Selected)

<ul class="pub-list">

**International Labour Organization**

<li>
*Promoviendo el trabajo decente para las trabajadoras del hogar de Perú: COVID-19, cambios en el empleo, condiciones laborales y riesgos de trabajo forzoso de las trabajadoras del hogar remuneradas en Perú.*
<span class="paper-links">
<a href="https://www.ilo.org/es/publications/promoviendo-el-trabajo-decente-para-las-trabajadoras-del-hogar-de-peru" target="_blank">ILO</a>
</span>
<span class="pub-source">Organización Internacional del Trabajo, 2025.</span>
</li>
<li>
*Aprendiendo sobre la transversalización de la perspectiva de género: Avances y matices en los programas de trabajo de la OIT en América Latina y el Caribe, 2010–2021* (with A. Eid Valdiviezo and N. Echenique).
<span class="paper-links">
<a href="https://www.ilo.org/es/publications/aprendiendo-sobre-la-transversalizacion-de-la-perspectiva-de-genero-avances" target="_blank">ILO</a>
</span>
<span class="pub-source">Organización Internacional del Trabajo, 2022.</span>
</li>
<li>
*Meta-analysis of ILO interventions in Latin America and the Caribbean on child labor, forced labor and human trafficking.*
<span class="paper-links">
<a href="https://www.ilo.org/es/publications/meta-analisis-de-las-intervenciones-de-la-oit-en-america-latina-y-el-caribe" target="_blank">ILO</a>
</span>
<span class="pub-source">Organización Internacional del Trabajo, 2020.</span>
</li>
<li>
*Magnitud y Características del Trabajo Infantil en Bolivia: Informe Nacional 2008.*
<span class="paper-links">
<a href="https://www.ilo.org/sites/default/files/2025-05/nat_rep_bol.pdf" target="_blank">PDF</a>
</span>
<span class="pub-source">Organización Internacional del Trabajo, 2008.</span>
</li>

**Inter-American Development Bank**

<li>
*Intended and Unintended Effects of Non-contributory Pensions: The Case of Bolivia's Renta Dignidad* (with G. Mena).
<span class="paper-links">
<a href="https://publications.iadb.org/en/intended-and-unintended-effects-unconditional-cash-transfers-case-bolivias-renta-dignidad" target="_blank">IDB</a>
<a href="https://lajed.ucb.edu.bo/a/article/view/e108" target="_blank" class="paper-link-published">DOI</a>
</span>
<span class="pub-source">IDB Working Paper No. IDB-WP-631, 2015. Also in *Revista Latinoamericana de Desarrollo Económico*, 2015.</span>
</li>
<li>
*Unemployment in Bolivia: Risks and Labor Market Policies* (with E. Yáñez and M.A. Villegas).
<span class="paper-links">
<a href="https://publications.iadb.org/en/unemployment-bolivia-risks-and-labor-market-policies" target="_blank">IDB</a>
</span>
<span class="pub-source">IDB Working Paper Series No. IDB-WP-271, 2011.</span>
</li>

**World Bank · IDB · UNDP — Book Chapters**

<li>
*Are Teachers Well Paid in Latin America and the Caribbean?* In *Incentives to Improve Teaching: Lessons from Latin America*, E. Vegas (Ed.).
<span class="pub-source">World Bank, 2005.</span>
</li>
<li>
*Regional Integration and Wage Inequality* (with S. Duryea and R. Robertson). In *Beyond Borders: 2002 Report on Economic and Social Progress in Latin America*.
<span class="pub-source">Inter-American Development Bank, 2002.</span>
</li>
<li>
*Bolivia: Efectos de la Liberalización sobre el Crecimiento, Empleo, Distribución del Ingreso y Pobreza* (with R. Pereira and W. Jiménez). In *Liberalización, Desigualdad y Pobreza: América Latina y el Caribe en los 90*.
<span class="pub-source">UNDP, 2002.</span>
</li>

**Bolivia — Survey Experiments**

<li>
*¿Qué Separa a los Bolivianos? Un Estudio Experimental de los Efectos de las Identidades Sociales en la Confianza y el Afecto.*
<span class="paper-links">
<a href="https://lajed.ucb.edu.bo/a/article/view/541" target="_blank" class="paper-link-published">DOI</a>
<a href="https://github.com/wernerhl/working-papers/raw/main/que_separa_bolivianos_slides.pptx" target="_blank">Slides (PPTX)</a>
<a href="https://github.com/wernerhl/working-papers/raw/main/que_separa_bolivianos_slides.key" target="_blank">Slides (Keynote)</a>
</span>
<span class="pub-source">*Revista Latinoamericana de Desarrollo Económico*, 2024.</span>
</li>
<li>
*Understanding Changes in Social Cohesion in Bolivia* (with M.A. Villegas).
<span class="paper-links">
<a href="https://www.aru.org.bo/REPEC/pdf/understanding-changes-in-social-cohesion-bolivia.pdf" target="_blank">PDF</a>
<a href="https://github.com/wernerhl/working-papers/raw/main/social_cohesion_bolivia_slides.pptx" target="_blank">Slides</a>
</span>
<span class="pub-source">International Conference on Social Cohesion and Development, OECD, 2010.</span>
</li>

**Impact Evaluation of Bolivian Social Programs** — *Revista Latinoamericana de Desarrollo Económico*, 2015.

<li>
*Intended and Unintended Effects of Unconditional Cash Transfers: The Case of Bolivia's Renta Dignidad* (with G. Mena).
<span class="paper-links">
<a href="https://lajed.ucb.edu.bo/a/article/view/e108" target="_blank" class="paper-link-published">DOI</a>
</span>
<span class="pub-source">77–148.</span>
</li>
<li>
*Temporary Employment Programs, Consumption Smoothing and Employability.*
<span class="paper-links">
<a href="https://lajed.ucb.edu.bo/a/article/view/e106" target="_blank" class="paper-link-published">DOI</a>
</span>
<span class="pub-source">7–34.</span>
</li>
<li>
*Job Training and Employability: Evidence from Mi Primer Empleo Digno* (with P. Villarroel).
<span class="paper-links">
<a href="https://lajed.ucb.edu.bo/a/article/view/e107" target="_blank" class="paper-link-published">DOI</a>
</span>
<span class="pub-source">35–76.</span>
</li>
<li>
*Free of Illiteracy? Evaluating the Bolivian Experience with the National Literacy Program Yo Sí Puedo* (with P. Villarroel and C. Valencia).
<span class="paper-links">
<a href="https://lajed.ucb.edu.bo/a/article/view/e109" target="_blank" class="paper-link-published">DOI</a>
</span>
<span class="pub-source">149–232.</span>
</li>
<li>
*Evaluating the Impact of Micro-credit in Bolivia* (with P. Villarroel).
<span class="paper-links">
<a href="https://lajed.ucb.edu.bo/a/article/view/e110" target="_blank" class="paper-link-published">DOI</a>
</span>
<span class="pub-source">233–282.</span>
</li>
<li>
*Assessing the Impact of School Subsidies in Bolivia: A Reduced Form Non-Parametric Approach.*
<span class="paper-links">
<a href="https://lajed.ucb.edu.bo/a/article/view/e101" target="_blank" class="paper-link-published">DOI</a>
</span>
<span class="pub-source">283–296.</span>
</li>

**Poverty and Inequality in Bolivia**

<li>
*La evolución de la pobreza en Bolivia: un enfoque multidimensional* (with P. Villarroel).
<span class="paper-links">
<a href="https://lajed.ucb.edu.bo/a/article/view/a126" target="_blank" class="paper-link-published">DOI</a>
</span>
<span class="pub-source">*Revista Latinoamericana de Desarrollo Económico*, No. 20, 2013, 7–74.</span>
</li>
</ul>
